note: PR Freelance pipeline scrape May 2-8, 2026. 6 fresh leads, all 55+ combined. Headline lead is Infinite Global, which acquired Dukas Linden Public Relations on May 6, 2026 — a top-10 financial PR shop — creating immediate post-merger integration overflow on Mark's exact ICP (asset/wealth mgmt, capital markets, fintech, blockchain/crypto, professional services). Five additional agency-overflow targets across financial services PR. No duplicates of pr-freelance-scrape-2026-04-30 through 2026-05-07 (Talent Table, Vested, Inkhouse, Rho, Kaia Investment Partners, Reveille Advisors, Vanta, Deel, Stripe Health, Anthropic, Runway, Rain, Tether, Capital One/Discover, Offchain Labs, GlossGenius, Upstart, Siege Media, Mission North, BVNK, Coinbase/Base, Atticus, Cloudbeds, FactSet, Robinhood) or any daily-scrape leads. Job-board postings sourced via web search of Google/Bing indexed results — no LinkedIn bot scraping.

leads.name: Infinite Global
leads.industry: Strategic Communications & Reputation Management — Financial Services Platform (post-Dukas Linden acquisition)
leads.location: Transatlantic — New York, London, San Francisco, Chicago
leads.compensationText: Not stated — agency-overflow engagement (project / hourly / monthly retainer typical for white-label work)
leads.employmentTypeRaw: Cold approach — agency overflow / white-label senior consultant
leads.notes: HEADLINE LEAD this week (combined 82, A grade). Infinite (transatlantic comms / reputation mgmt firm, ParkSouth Ventures-backed) closed acquisition of Dukas Linden Public Relations on 2026-05-06. DLPR is consistently top-10 in O'Dwyer's financial PR rankings; clients sit across asset & wealth management, capital markets, fintech, blockchain/crypto, and professional services — a near-perfect overlap with Mark's ICP. Post-merger integration phase + PE growth mandate = immediate freelance overflow demand for senior fin-comms operators who can plug in without onboarding lift. Pitch: time-boxed cold outreach to Diaferia, Olsen, and Linden in the next 2 weeks while capacity decisions are still fluid. Lead with: 1) crisis/issues case studies in financial services, 2) capacity to absorb integration-period account work white-label, 3) flexible engagement model (hourly + project + retainer all on the table). The merger window is the buy signal — once teams are restructured this opportunity calcifies.

leads.name: The Work Crowd (Fintech PR Network)
leads.industry: Vetted Freelance PR Network — Fintech / Financial Services Specialist
leads.compensationText: Day rate / project — set by freelancer when registering
leads.source: The Work Crowd Fintech PR landing page
leads.notes: Pipeline gateway lead (combined 75, high B). Not a single buyer — a vetted-freelancer network that places senior PR consultants into fintech corporate and startup engagements. Mark's edge: senior strategist + executor profile differentiates from junior freelancer pool. Action: register (free), build a tight fintech-only profile, list 3-5 anchor case studies (regulatory framing, capital markets narrative, crisis comms in payments). Once vetted, inbound routing is automatic — this is a low-effort recurring-flow play, not a single pitch. Stack with direct outreach to Vested / Caliber / William Mills as the active channel.

leads.name: William Mills Agency
leads.industry: PR Agency — Largest Independent Firm Dedicated Exclusively to Financial Services & Fintech
leads.employmentTypeRaw: Cold approach + open positions on careers page
leads.notes: Strong agency-overflow target (combined 65, B grade). WMA bills itself as the largest independent PR firm dedicated exclusively to financial services and fintech — ~50 years in market, deep media-relations bench, broad fintech client mix. Atlanta-headquartered but operates nationally with remote-friendly hybrid model. Mark's pitch: senior fin-comms operator with NYC/national media relationships available for project, retainer, or campaign-specific overflow. Approach William Mills III directly + a separate ping to ops/talent lead. Lead with 1-page capabilities + 3 fintech earned-media samples; offer a 30-day pilot to prove drop-in capability.

leads.name: Caliber Corporate Advisers
leads.industry: PR Agency — Fintech, Financial Services, Insurance / InsurTech, Real Estate / PropTech
leads.employmentTypeRaw: Cold approach + open positions on careers page
leads.notes: Tight ICP fit (combined 63, B grade). Caliber is a NYC boutique covering fintech, insurance/InsurTech, real estate/PropTech, and broader financial services — three of the four are exactly Mark's ICP. Marketing + PR integrated offering = breadth of overflow needs (earned media, content, executive comms, thought leadership). Pitch: position as a senior fin-comms drop-in, available for retainer or per-account overflow; reference Mark's wealth/asset/fintech samples. Approach via LinkedIn cold message to founder + careers page application as parallel paths.

leads.name: 5WPR (Financial Services & Fintech Practice)
leads.industry: PR Agency — Financial Services & Fintech Practice (VC, PE, asset managers, fintech operators)
leads.employmentTypeRaw: Cold outbound to financial services practice lead
leads.notes: Solid investor/PE practice fit (combined 60, B grade). 5WPR's disclosed client roster includes Standard General, BlueRun Ventures, P&G Ventures, and Insight Partners — buyer-quality VC/PE accounts that match Mark's ICP exactly. Practice also runs SPAC/IPO communications. 5WPR is a mid-size agency, so overflow happens at the practice-line level rather than firm-wide; outreach should target the financial services practice lead directly, not generic careers email. Pitch: senior IR/PE/fintech narrative operator available for retainer or per-account drop-in. Reference Mark's narrative + media-list strength specifically for VC/PE founder positioning.

leads.name: Cognito Media (NYC Fintech PR)
leads.industry: PR Agency — Fintech & Financial Services Specialist (early-stage to exit)
leads.employmentTypeRaw: Cold approach to NYC office + careers page
leads.source: Cognito Media NYC fintech PR landing page
leads.notes: Niche fintech-specialist agency (combined 58, B grade). Cognito Media is a global fintech-focused PR shop with NYC, London, and Singapore offices — they explicitly position around taking fintech clients from elevator pitch through strategic comms and exit. Mark's pitch: senior fintech narrative + media operator available for cross-office overflow (NYC media-relations work that London/Singapore teams can't cover). Smaller pipeline opportunity than Infinite or William Mills but tight thematic alignment — file as a tier-2 secondary outreach in the next 2-3 weeks.
